title: Completely-positive non-signalling non-Markovian dynamics
abstract: We define non-Markovian quantum dynamics as evolution in which the current state depends on all past states, and completely characterize its structure under the assumptions of complete positivity and non-signalling. The resulting continuous-time dynamics is an integro-differential equation that augments the Gorini-Kossakowski-Sudarshan-Lindblad equation with a memory integral, and is capable of describing the quantum state of systems exposed to noise with any integrable power spectral density with no further approximations. We then establish a formalism to evaluate multi-time correlations of measurement outcomes in this general setting, obviating the need for a regression theorem. As an application, we derive the emission spectrum of a driven two-level system coupled to a non-Markovian bath: the familiar Mollow triplet acquires a frequency-dependent linewidth that encodes the memory of the bath. Our work provides a rigorous yet transparent description of the quantum state of non-Markovian systems, opening the door for state estimation and state-based quantum control beyond the Markovian regime.
summary: This paper develops a general mathematical framework for continuous-time quantum dynamics that accounts for memory effects while preserving complete positivity and non-signaling. It introduces a new integro-differential master equation that generalizes the Lindblad equation to include an integral term for bath memory. The authors demonstrate the utility of this approach by showing how non-Markovianity manifests as frequency-dependent linewidths in the Mollow triplet of a driven two-level system.
main_problem: The lack of a general, model-agnostic, and mathematically consistent framework for continuous-time non-Markovian quantum dynamics that satisfies both complete positivity and the non-signaling principle.
main_result: The derivation of a new integro-differential master equation that generalizes the GKSL equation with a memory integral, capable of describing systems coupled to noise with any integrable power spectral density.
method: The authors use convex analysis and functional analysis to take the continuous-time limit of a discrete-time non-Markovian quantum stochastic process, applying the Wiener-Hopf factorization method for analytical solutions.
model_or_system: A general quantum system undergoing non-Markovian evolution, with a specific application to a driven two-level system (TLS) coupled to a stationary bosonic bath.
key_observables: Two-time correlation functions of measurement outcomes, measurement currents, and the emission spectrum (specifically the Mollow triplet).
important_parameters: Integrable power spectral density, strong driving/detuning limits, and the distinction between Markovian and non-Markovian rates.
main_assumptions: The framework assumes complete positivity and non-signaling; it neglects system-bath correlations (Born approximation) and the back-action of the system on the bath, and is limited to integrable power spectral densities.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates a physical realization of the proposed dynamics using a system and bath evolving via a joint Markovian map.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining non-Markovianity and establishing the mathematical framework via discrete-time maps, derives the continuous-time integro-differential master equation using convex analysis, applies the formalism to a driven two-level system to analyze the Mollow triplet, and concludes with discussion on limitations and future work.
why_interesting: It provides a rigorous, first-principles derivation of a non-Markovian master equation that avoids ad-hoc approximations, offering a powerful tool for state estimation and control in open quantum systems subject to complex noise.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
